1781.description: Null orgId bypasses org-scoping check (fail-open) — if (orgId && task.organizationId !== orgId) skips guard when orgId is undefined
1781.reason: verified — guard tightened to !orgId || ... matching fail-closed pattern in code-review.ts/epics.ts/kg.ts/stack.ts

1781.description: Forbidden response uses jsonResponse instead of errorResponse — missing isError: true flag
1781.reason: verified — switched to errorResponse() and updated test assertion that codified the bug

1781.description: Redundant inner userId/orgId declarations shadow identical outer-scope declarations at lines 124-125
1781.reason: verified — outer and inner are identical except quoting; inner removed

1781.description: Identical null-org guard duplicated 3x across record_review_started, record_finding, record_review_completed
1781.reason: extracted requireOrgMatch() helper; all 3 handlers now share one implementation

1781.description: Double-quote string "anonymous" breaks codebase single-quote convention

1782.description: get_epic uses `!epicId` + `'missing_param'` with cast `as string`, while list_tasks/get_task use `!id || id.trim() === ''` + domain-specific error codes and `as string | undefined`. Whitespace-only epic_id bypasses validation and produces a misleading not_found error
1782.reason: Fixed in 6189e0e7 — cast/guard/error-code aligned with siblings

1782.description: Type cast `as string` differs from sibling handlers' `as string | undefined`
1782.reason: Folded into the MAJOR fix in 6189e0e7

1782.description: Task-ID detection regex `/^[A-Za-z0-9]+-\d+-\d+/` lacks `$` end-anchor — strings like AUTH-1-3-extra match and are mis-routed to get_task
1782.reason: Fixed in 6189e0e7 — added `$` anchor, plus regression test for trailing-character routing

1782.description: Regex prefix `[A-Za-z0-9]+` allows numeric-only prefixes (e.g. 123-1-1) which would be routed as task IDs
1782.reason: Pre-existing pattern, tightening risks behavior change for callers with numeric-prefixed keys; tracked separately

1782.description: Test asserts `payload.error === 'missing_param'` mirroring the divergent impl error code; must be updated in lockstep with the impl fix
1782.reason: Fixed in 6189e0e7 — assertion updated to 'missing_epic_id', plus a new whitespace-only test case

1782.description: Validation trims epic_id to detect whitespace-only strings, but the untrimmed value is then passed to stateManager.getEpic — `  AUTH-1  ` clears the guard but fails the lookup
1782.reason: Fixed in 1c1d2647 — switched to `(args.X as string|undefined)?.trim()` across get_epic/list_tasks/get_task; pre-existing bug shared by all three

1782.description: mockStateManager, makeDeps(), and session-context mock are copy-pasted between epics-list.test.ts and epics-tool.test.ts
1782.reason: Test-infrastructure refactor outside the 'logic errors' scope of this PR; tracking separately

1782.description: Typed parseResponse() helper in epics-list.test.ts vs inline JSON.parse(result.content[0].text) in epics-tool.test.ts
1782.reason: Same scope as the duplicate-fixtures refactor — defer to a shared-helper extraction

1783.description: error code 'missing_params' (plural) diverges from sibling tools — index.ts and stack.ts use singular 'missing_param'
1783.reason: normalized to singular across all three sites; updated matching test assertion + description

1783.description: unused type/value imports — round-1 flagged getCurrentOrgId/getCurrentUserId, round-2 flagged EpicTask
1783.reason: already fixed at HEAD by commit 91def016 (resolve lint error in skills.test.ts) before this triage; current HEAD has no unused imports

1783.description: test description says 'forbidden error response for unknown tool' but assertion checks 'unknown_tool' — misleading

1785.description: recordFinding writes undefined Firestore field values — startLine/endLine/suggestion use ?? undefined; Firestore rejects unless ignoreUndefinedProperties is enabled
1785.reason: db.ts:21 sets ignoreUndefinedProperties:true globally — Firestore silently drops undefined, no rejection occurs

1785.description: createReview writes undefined Firestore field values — executionId/skillId use ?? undefined
1785.reason: same as [1] — global ignoreUndefinedProperties handles it

1785.description: listPullRequests filters need composite Firestore indexes for repository.fullName and author.username with full sort order
1785.reason: the exact indexes the bot says are missing are present in firestore.indexes.json

1785.description: no-filter list query has no matching composite index (caught during verification of [3])
1785.reason: added (orgId ASC, openedAt DESC, id DESC) for default-sort path

1785.description: unbounded collectionGroup('reviews').get() loads everything into memory
1785.reason: replaced with db.collection('pullRequests').listDocuments() bounded enumeration

1785.description: N+1 query pattern in recomputeParentPr — each review triggers a separate findings read
1785.reason: parallelized finding fetch with Promise.all

1785.description: COLLECTION_GROUP indexes lack orgId field for defense-in-depth
1785.reason: re-added orgId as leading field on all three pullRequests indexes; updated listPullRequests to add explicit where('orgId',==,X) clause

1785.description: stale comment references top-level pullRequests collection
1785.reason: updated comment to reflect org-scoped storage

1785.description: completeReview reads findings outside transaction — worstSeverity may be stale
1785.reason: expanded comment to document hasFindingsMin monotonicity invariant and recordFinding's atomic update

1785.description: patchFinding uses non-atomic get-then-update (pre-existing)
1785.reason: pre-existing — bot itself flags it; out of scope for this migration

1785.description: BASE_PR mock uses old flat PullRequest shape — repoFullName/author string/reviewCount at top level
1785.reason: rewrote mock to canonical PullRequest shape with nested repository/author/stats

1785.description: individual Firestore writes instead of batched writes
1785.reason: one-shot migration on small data (~10 docs in prod) — batching is optimization, not correctness

1785.description: pr.repository.fullName without optional chaining
1785.reason: PullRequest.repository is required (not nullable) on the shared interface; webhook always writes it; verified in prod for PR 1781. Adding ?. contradicts the type contract

1785.description: pr.repository.fullName without optional chaining
1785.reason: same as #12 — type-required field, writer guarantees presence

1785.description: MOCK_PR_2 inherits stale url ending in /pull/42 for PR number 43
1785.reason: added url override to MOCK_PR_2

1785.description: COLLECTION_GROUP indexes won't serve COLLECTION queries — listPullRequests will fail with missing-index errors in prod despite emulator tests passing
1785.reason: verified — prCollection(orgId) is COLLECTION-scoped; index scope must match

1785.description: copyReview returns copied:true and findingsCopied++ runs in dry-run — operator could trust dry-run output and proceed to --delete-source on data that was never copied
1785.reason: verified — both counters and return value were unguarded

1785.description: deleteSourceTree deletes findings one-by-one then deletes review doc; partial failure orphans data
1785.reason: data-loss claim is inaccurate (copyFindings runs before deleteSourceTree at line 299), but batch atomicity is a small genuine robustness improvement; fixed via db.batch()

1785.description: hardcoded path strings duplicate manager constants
1785.reason: one-shot migration script, retired post-run; not worth the churn

1785.description: recomputeParentPr uses review.id from data instead of reviewDoc.id
1785.reason: backfill processes legacy data — doc key is the right source of truth

1785.description: recomputeParentPr fans out finding reads with no concurrency cap
1785.reason: one-shot script, ~10 docs in prod per bot's own admission — no real risk

1785.description: parentPrsRecomputed counter increments outside APPLY guard
1785.reason: same bug class as [2]; fixed together

1785.description: getPR returns raw data without injecting snap.id as fallback
1785.reason: trivial fix — switched to toPullRequest(snap) helper that getPullRequest already uses

1785.description: completeReview computes findings stats outside transaction; concurrent recordFinding can produce stale findingCount
1785.reason: design intent already documented at lines 1140-1156 with the exact trade-off bot raises; suggested fix would regress clarity

1785.description: mixed dot-notation and top-level field update style
1785.reason: mixing required by Firestore — dot-notation prevents overwriting nested objects via shallow merge; cannot be made consistent without changing data shape

1788.description: formatDuration produces '-1m' for negative ms (clock skew on firstReviewLatencyMs)
1788.reason: extracted to ui/src/utils/formatDuration.ts with ms<=0 guard returning '< 1m'
partial — extracted to shared util and consumed here. Other 8 files defer to a follow-up because signatures vary
identical claim is wrong — UsageDashboard's has K/M compact abbreviations, ours is plain toLocaleString. Created shared util for this page; UsageDashboard kept as-is because semantics differ

1788.description: actionableCount treats findings with status===undefined as actionable
1788.reason: current code is correct — the existing dashboard treats undefined as 'pending' (line 366: f.status??'pending'); changing would create mismatch with status-filter pill counts

1788.description: stray leading space in diff stats when additions is null and deletions is present
1788.reason: moved separator inside additions+deletions conjunction conditional

1788.description: onRunAnotherReview is permanently disabled stub with no tracking issue
1788.reason: added inline comment referencing punch-list item 4 in PR description

1788.description: earliestReviewStart and latestReviewStart each do .map().reduce() over the same array
1788.reason: real on tiny array (<=30 reviews per cap); two-pass code is more readable than Math.min/max spread which needs empty-array fallback

1788.description: Sub-minute durations (0 < ms < 60_000) bypass <= 0 guard and return '0m'
1788.reason: replaced <= 0 guard with < 60_000 to cover all three degenerate cases

1788.description: Invalid startedAt → getTime() returns NaN → reduce propagates → new Date(NaN).toISOString() throws RangeError
1788.reason: filter Number.isFinite before Math.min/max — NaN can't reach toISOString()

1788.description: NaN input falls through every comparison and returns 'NaNd'
1788.reason: added Number.isFinite guard at top returning '—'

1788.description: Doc comment claims 9 other files have local copies but count is unverified/drifted
1788.reason: removed specific count from docstring — now says 'other files' without naming a number
rewrote example to → '< 1m' only; added explicit examples for 0, negative, and NaN

1788.description: <h1> replaced with <span> — page loses its only heading, breaking a11y
1788.reason: restored <h1 class=crpd-header-title> wrapping PR # and title; added margin:0 to keep layout identical

1788.description: formatDuration returns 0m for sub-minute positive values
1788.reason: already fixed in round-2 commit b3a8c03f via < 60_000 guard

1788.description: actionableCount uses subtraction instead of explicit status filter
1788.reason: intentional — undefined-status findings are pending (line 366 f.status ?? pending), bot's filter would exclude them and break meta/pill count agreement
already fixed in round-2 commit b3a8c03f via rewritten example block

1788.description: Run another review button always disabled, shipped as dead UI
1788.reason: already addressed in round-1 commit 2778a438 via inline TODO comment referencing punch-list item 4

1803.description: Transient PR fetch error permanently drops review_completed event — caught errors silently early-return without throwing
1803.reason: re-throw on caught errors so the caller's .catch() surfaces the failure for retry; null-PR early return preserved as the genuine data-integrity case

1803.description: publishFinding re-fetches the parent PR on every call — N findings = N identical Firestore reads
1803.reason: cross-cutting refactor; same codepath touched by #1797 — better to do both denormalisation changes together

1803.description: Inline magic number 60 for BQ summary truncation — every other BQ default is a named constant
1803.reason: extracted BQ_SUMMARY_MAX_LENGTH near the other DEFAULT_* constants

1803.description: buildRepositoryStruct inconsistent ?? null — full_name and platform omitted
1803.reason: added ?? null to all five fields for parity; comment explains defence against schema drift

1803.description: sha='unknown' writes invalid placeholder to BQ — 7 chars vs 40-char hex
1803.reason: extracted UNKNOWN_SHA constant; can't switch to null because sha is REQUIRED on both BQ tables — comment documents the trade-off
